title: Create the Gapminder dataset
text:
# Import necessary libraries
import pandas as pd
from gapminder import gapminder

# Rename 'lifeExp' to 'life_expectancy' and 'gdpPercap' to 'gdp_per_capita'
gapminder = gapminder.rename(columns={'lifeExp': 'life_expectancy',
                                      'pop': 'population_millions', 
                                      'gdpPercap': 'gdp_per_capita'})

# Convert population to millions
gapminder['population_millions'] = gapminder['population_millions'] / 1_000_000

# Create a new pandas DataFrame from the modified gapminder data
gapminder_df = pd.DataFrame(gapminder)

# Save the DataFrame as a CSV file
gapminder_df.to_csv('gapminder.csv', index=False)

# Print the first 3 rows of the DataFrame
print(gapminder_df.head(3))

       country continent  year  life_expectancy  population_millions  \
0  Afghanistan      Asia  1952           28.801             8.425333   
1  Afghanistan      Asia  1957           30.332             9.240934   
2  Afghanistan      Asia  1962           31.997            10.267083   

   gdp_per_capita  
0      779.445314  
1      820.853030  
2      853.100710

title: Life Expectancy and GDP per Capita
text:
This page explores how life expectancy changes as GDP per capita increases, using the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load dataset
df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

# Check column names
df.columns

Index(['country', 'continent', 'year', 'life_expectancy',
       'population_millions', 'gdp_per_capita'],
      dtype='object')



# Scatter plot: GDP per capita vs Life expectancy
plt.figure()
plt.scatter(df["gdp_per_capita"], df["life_expectancy"])
plt.xlabel("GDP per Capita")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy")
plt.title("Life Expectancy vs GDP per Capita")
plt.show()

title: Life Expectancy and GDP per Capita
text:
This page explores how life expectancy changes as GDP per capita increases, using the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load dataset
df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

# Check column names
df.columns

Index(['country', 'continent', 'year', 'life_expectancy',
       'population_millions', 'gdp_per_capita'],
      dtype='object')



# Scatter plot: GDP per capita vs Life expectancy
plt.figure()
plt.scatter(df["gdp_per_capita"], df["life_expectancy"])
plt.xlabel("GDP per Capita")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy")
plt.title("Life Expectancy vs GDP per Capita")
plt.show()

title: Life Expectancy and GDP per Capita
text:
Interpretation
The scatterplot suggests that as GDP per capita increases, life expectancy tends to increase as well. The relationship flattens for higher-income countries. This indicates diminishing returns at higher GDP levels.

title: Country Analysis
text:
This page explores how life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population change over time for a single country in the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load dataset
df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

# Check column names
df.columns

Index(['country', 'continent', 'year', 'life_expectancy',
       'population_millions', 'gdp_per_capita'],
      dtype='object')



# choosing US as country
country_name = "United States"

country_df = df[df["country"] == country_name].sort_values("year")

country_df.head()

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))

# Life expectancy
plt.subplot(3, 1, 1)
plt.plot(country_df["year"], country_df["life_expectancy"])
plt.title(f"{country_name}: Trends Over Time")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy")

# GDP per capita
plt.subplot(3, 1, 2)
plt.plot(country_df["year"], country_df["gdp_per_capita"])
plt.ylabel("GDP per Capita")

# Population
plt.subplot(3, 1, 3)
plt.plot(country_df["year"], country_df["population_millions"])
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Population (millions)")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

title: Country Analysis
text:
This page explores how life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population change over time for a single country in the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load dataset
df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

# Check column names
df.columns

Index(['country', 'continent', 'year', 'life_expectancy',
       'population_millions', 'gdp_per_capita'],
      dtype='object')



# choosing US as country
country_name = "United States"

country_df = df[df["country"] == country_name].sort_values("year")

country_df.head()

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))

# Life expectancy
plt.subplot(3, 1, 1)
plt.plot(country_df["year"], country_df["life_expectancy"])
plt.title(f"{country_name}: Trends Over Time")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy")

# GDP per capita
plt.subplot(3, 1, 2)
plt.plot(country_df["year"], country_df["gdp_per_capita"])
plt.ylabel("GDP per Capita")

# Population
plt.subplot(3, 1, 3)
plt.plot(country_df["year"], country_df["population_millions"])
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Population (millions)")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

title: Country Analysis
text:
Interpretation
For the United States, life expectancy generally increases over time, GDP per capita significantly rises, and population steadily increases as well. This indicates economic and health improvements over time.

title: Life Expectancy and Population
text:
This page explores how life expectancy relates to population size in the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load dataset
df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

# Check column names
df.columns

Index(['country', 'continent', 'year', 'life_expectancy',
       'population_millions', 'gdp_per_capita'],
      dtype='object')



# Scatter plot: Population vs Life expectancy
plt.figure()
plt.scatter(df["population_millions"], df["life_expectancy"])
plt.xlabel("Population (millions)")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy")
plt.title("Life Expectancy vs Population")
plt.show()

title: Life Expectancy and Population
text:
This page explores how life expectancy relates to population size in the Gapminder dataset.

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load dataset
df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

# Check column names
df.columns

Index(['country', 'continent', 'year', 'life_expectancy',
       'population_millions', 'gdp_per_capita'],
      dtype='object')



# Scatter plot: Population vs Life expectancy
plt.figure()
plt.scatter(df["population_millions"], df["life_expectancy"])
plt.xlabel("Population (millions)")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy")
plt.title("Life Expectancy vs Population")
plt.show()

title: Life Expectancy and Population
text:
Interpretation
The scatterplot shows that there isn’t a strong linear relationship between population size and life expectancy. Unlike GDP per capita, population alone does not appear to strongly predict life expectancy.
